lines.content: “ Those who can imagine anything, can create the impossible.” - Alan Turing
“ Sometimes it is the people who no one imagined anything of who do the things that no one can imagine” - Alan Turing
“ Logic will get you from A to Z; imagination will get you everywhere.” - Albert Einstein
“ Anyone who has never made a mistake has never tried anything new.” - Albert Einstein
“ Any sufficiently advanced technology is equivalent to magic.” - Arthur C. Clarke
“ If it's a good idea, go ahead and do it. It's much easier to apologize than it is to get permission.” - Grace Hopper
“ Programs must be written for people to read, and only incidentally for machines to execute.” - Harold Abelson

* Note: Refer to input.txt in text-ui-test folder to see examples of the following errors
* 1. Commands are not case sensitive (ie List will be recognised as list)
* 3. Missing / Wrong keyword for the wrong command (e.g. using start/end for deadline)
* 4. Missing description / date (for deadline / start & end)
* 5. Mark/Unmark/Delete item number out of range (task index does not exist in list)
* 6. Mark/Unmark/Delete invalid task number (e.g. mark 2a, mark   , mark -1)
* 7. Mark task that has been marked (& vice versa)
* 8. Date and time format not valid
* 9. For event, Start Date after End Date
// TAKES IN DD-MM-YYYY prints out MMM dd YYYY
* Initialises ui, storage, parser and tasklist (loads old data in hard disk using storage object)
* If no old data, creates empty tasklist
tasks = new TaskList(storage.load());    // load prev tasks
* All exceptions raised in Command Classes are classified as Duck Exception
* Allows Duck Class to only have to catch DuckException even though there are multiple types of 'errors'
* Each task is one Item object
public Item(String text, LocalDate firstDate, LocalTime firstTime){
public Item(String text, LocalDate firstDate, LocalTime firstTime, LocalDate secondDate, LocalTime secondTime){
* Helper function for string formatting LocalDate datatype
* Used by toString() and toStringFile() methods
* @return a string (which will be used in printing to console + storing to hard disk) -- "null" or "Mar 23 2024"
* Helper function for string formatting LocalTime datatype
* Used by toString() and toStringFile() methods
* @return a string (which will be used in printing to console + storing to hard disk) -- "null" or "12:00pm"
* String format all information about Item to save on hard disk
* @return a string e.g. T | 0 | Do Homework or E | 0 | School | 24-02-2025 | 13:00 | 24-02-2025 | 18:00
* Getter method for done --> Identify if a task is marked / unmarked
* Helper function to convert getDone boolean info into a printable string for toString method
* Helper function for toStringFile which formats 'done' (mark/unmark) as 1 or 0
* @return string (either "1" if done=true or "0" if done=false)
* @param tasktype (which is an enum ToDo, Deadline or Event)
* Helper function to convert getType enum info into a printable string for toString method
* Class which makes sense of user input
* Interacts with UI to obtain commands + print information to terminal
* Main Code that processes user input
* @return Command object --> cld be todo, deadline, event, delete, mark etc.
* @throws DuckException Self-defined Exception Class which identifies error (using error message)
public Command parse(String command) throws DuckException{
throw new DuckException("ERROR! Mark/Unmark command must have an integer index behind");
throw new DuckException("ERROR! Description of Task cannot be empty");  // to catch cases where only todo deadline event are the input
if (command.contains("todo")) {  // no date or time attached
return new TodoCommand(byDatetimePos, startDatetimePos, endDatetimePos, subCommand);
return new DeadlineCommand(byDatetimePos, startDatetimePos, endDatetimePos, subCommand);
return new EventCommand(byDatetimePos, startDatetimePos, endDatetimePos, subCommand);
throw new DuckException("ERROR! Delete command must have an integer index behind");
throw new DuckException("ERROR! Find command must have keyword(s) behind");

* Manages a list of all tasks

totalStr = totalStr + "\tNow you have " + this.size() + " tasks in the list" + '\n';
* Deletes task at index index from list
totalStr = totalStr + "\tQuack! I've removed this task:" + '\n';
totalStr = totalStr + "\tNow you have " + this.size() + " tasks in the list" + '\n';
* returns number of tasks in list

totalStr = totalStr + "\tAww! I've marked this task as not done yet:\n";
throw new IllegalArgumentException(("Item Already Marked as Not Done!"));
* Traverse through tasks tasklist and extract Items with the keyword word
* Defines enum such that TaskType can only be Todo, Deadline or Event
* Constructor Class --> Initiate Scanner Object to obtain User Input
System.out.println("Quack! I'm Duck.\nWhat can I do for you?");
System.out.println("1. list : Print out task list");
System.out.println("2. mark X : Mark task X (integer) as done");
System.out.println("3. unmark X : Mark task X (integer) as not done");
System.out.println("4. todo task : Add tasks without any date/time attached to it");
System.out.println("5. deadline task by DATE TIME: Add task that needs to be done by a specific date/time");
System.out.println("6. event task start DATE1 TIME1 end DATE2 TIME2 : Add task that start and end at specific dates/times");
System.out.println("7. delete X : Delete task X (integer) from list");
System.out.println("DATE format: DD-MM-YYYY \t TIME format: HH:MM (24-hr clock)");
System.out.println("May input 1) TIME ONLY ; 2) DATE ONLY ; 3) TIME & DATE");
* No previously stored data in Hard Disk
System.out.println("No Previously stored tasks. Creating New TaskList");
System.out.println("\tBye Quack! Hope to see you again soon!");
